mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts drives corporate revenue growth expectations, producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Tight monetary policy combined with rising unemployment creates a stagflationary shock that forces rapid portfolio de-risking, generating negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and UNRATE yields a positive 5-day return in more than 58% of observations over a rolling 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals a demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payroll employment directly increases aggregate consumption capacity and corporate revenue projections, generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive correlation with 20-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid labor market normalization that boosts equity risk appetite over one week.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines during recovery phases fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with signal accuracy dropping below 50% in walk-forward tests.
